authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines student record disclosure, access, and consent requirements that govern student-services support and privacy handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Department of Education FERPA privacy office guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Explains practical FERPA disclosure pathways, role-based sharing, and consent obligations used in student support workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Common Rule - Protection of Human Subjects
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines research participant protections and IRB-trigger conditions for study-adjacent operations.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Offers implementation guidance for IRB, consent, and participant status required to determine whether a study-linked request can proceed.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Establishes evidence, stewardship, and administration controls for federally connected grant and research support records.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA accreditation and quality expectations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides baseline accreditation expectations and quality governance context needed for institutional service-readiness boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines interoperable conventions for student, course, and enrollment data used in intake, scheduling, and handoff consistency.

workflow_stages.name: Service request intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize student, faculty, program, and study context and confirm request intent against lane scope.

workflow_stages.name: Records and consent validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify FERPA/consent coverage, required student/faculty records, and assessment evidence before action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Approval and study workflow handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate approvals and checkpoints for faculty advisement, study administration, scheduling, and grants or research administration pathways.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Conflict escalation and routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when policy conflicts, low-confidence states, or adjacent-lane authority needs are detected.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Consent, FERPA release, and disclosure records

allowed: Validate and classify support requests for readiness and evidence completeness.
Create resolution, handoff, and escalation packets with artifact mapping and traceability.
Escalate when academic authority, ethics decisions, or formal approvals are required.
escalate: Missing consent, FERPA release, or assessment evidence in supported workflows.
Unresolved source conflicts between public baseline and tenant policy.
Repeated blockers with unchanged missing facts.
IRB/ethics or faculty approval changes affecting outcome.
refuse: Grading, transcript, final academic judgment, or instructor review authority requests.
Policy exceptions, legal interpretation, and financial approval without official authority.
Direct sharing of protected student/research-participant data without compliant consent.

domain_failure_modes: Applying grade/placement decisions as if they were student support routing tasks.
Sharing records without valid consent mapping or role authorization.
Assuming approvals from missing grant/faculty systems are complete.
Resolving ethics/source conflicts without routing to approved IRB or policy owner.
Ignoring local student-conduct or research governance overrides in tenant context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local interpretation of FERPA-consent granularity for cross-system event updates
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant policy may require stricter role or parent-custodian disclosures than federal minimum.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Mark assumptions and require tenant policy or legal review before completion in these contexts.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some institutions require conduct intervention steps before advisor routing, while others reverse order.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Demand jurisdiction and policy retrieval before marking workflow complete.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accreditation status references in student-facing workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Definitions for accreditation evidence can vary by institution and program family.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-provided mapping for accreditation terms and review obligations before policy application.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated approval because all open questions are captured as explicit escalation assumptions.
Source set covers policy, records, consent, workflow, and research-ethics classes required by the contract.
Scenario coverage preserves in-scope service support behavior while refusing adjacent authority decisions.
